He's wearing all his outdoor essentials, including a harness with carabiners and some bright red gloves clipped on, ready for action. It looks like he's assembling or perhaps adjusting a piece of metal equipment, possibly a snow anchor or a measurement tool, which is crucial for our expedition. In the background, I can spot a blue snow shovel, confirming we're prepared for serious work in this snowy, mountainous environment. It was a bright, sunny day, perfect for getting things done while out on our w

photos.caption: Atelier Ancrage Alpin
photos.captionKii: Here's my friend, all bundled up in his bright orange jacket and black gloves, intently focused on the snow. He's down on one knee, carefully examining a snow pit or trench he's dug, which looks like an important step in assessing the snowpack. You can even spot his skis and a backpack in the background, hinting that we're on a serious winter expedition or perhaps a backcountry ski trip where understanding snow conditions is crucial for safety. It was a beautiful, sunny day on April 1st, perfect for some in-depth snow study out in the mountains.

photos.caption: img_4094.jpg
photos.captionKii: What a lovely day for a walk in the snowy woods! My friend is beaming, standing on a clear, snow-covered path, bundled up in a vibrant red jacket and a big backpack. Beside her, my happy dog, a beautiful light-colored retriever mix, is also smiling, his tail wagging as he looks at the camera. The forest around us is serene, with tall trees rising out of the snow, their branches mostly bare but still looking beautiful. It was a crisp, sunny day, perfect for enjoying the quiet beauty of nature and spending some quality time with my furry companion.

photos.caption: img_4085.jpg
photos.captionKii: What a fantastic day for some rock climbing! Here, one of my friends is making excellent progress up a rugged rock face, carefully finding handholds and footholds. He's wearing a white helmet and focused intently on the ascent, with ropes secured for safety. Below, another friend is expertly belaying, looking up and managing the rope, ensuring a safe climb. The sun is shining brightly, making for a clear blue sky, and although it's late March, you can still see some beautiful ice formations on the left, possibly a frozen waterfall, reminding us that winter was just fading away. The tall trees

photos.caption: img_4078.jpg
photos.captionKii: What a beautiful snowy day for skiing! Here are my friends enjoying our winter excursion, even with the snow actively falling around us. The guy in the foreground, with his big backpack and ski poles, is flashing a wide smile, clearly having a great time. Behind him, another friend is gliding along on her skis, bundled up in a blue jacket and a colorful hat, looking equally happy as she makes her way through the snow. The forest is absolutely picturesque with all the fresh snow clinging to the trees, making for such a serene backdrop to our adventure.

photos.caption: img_4069.jpg
photos.captionKii: We were really getting into the rhythm of our cross-country skiing adventure, finding some beautiful, open snowy areas alongside the forest. Here, one of my friends is gracefully gliding ahead, leaving fresh tracks in the pristine snow, while another follows behind. The bare trees on the right side of the frame give a stark contrast to the thick evergreens, highlighting the varied landscape we explored. It was another crisp, clear day, perfect for enjoying the quiet beauty of winter and the exhilaration of gliding through the snow.

photos.caption: img_4046.jpg
photos.captionKii: Here we are, deep in a snow-covered forest, making our way through the fresh powder on what looks like skis or snowshoes. It was another beautiful winter day, and my friends and I were enjoying the peaceful solitude of the woods. The tall evergreen trees, heavy with snow, lined the path as we trekked further in, our backpacks loaded for the adventure. You can see two of us in the foreground, and another friend further up the trail, all bundled up in our winter gear, navigating the snowy terrain. The fresh snow and quiet forest made for such a serene experience.

photos.caption: Winter hike for Barbara's BD
photos.captionKii: What a fantastic day out in the snowy woods! We took a break during our winter hike, and it looks like everyone was in good spirits. My friend on the left is holding his adorable fluffy white dog, who seems to be enjoying the fresh air as much as we are. In the middle, another friend is smiling and holding up a cool piece of fungus or wood he found, leaning casually against a snow-covered log. My friend on the right, wearing her sunglasses and a striped beanie, is also taking a load off with her blue backpack nestled beside her. The trees are bare, but the blanket of fresh snow made for such a

photos.caption: Lead Ice Climbing Course
photos.captionKii: Here we are, taking a moment to set up or perhaps discuss our anchor system in the snowy woods during our ice climbing adventure. I can see my friend on the left in the red helmet and dark jacket, another friend in the middle with the orange helmet and blue and yellow jacket, and another one on the right in the white helmet and dark gear. We're all in our climbing harnesses and winter boots, focused on the task at hand. The purple webbing, secured around these sturdy trees, is a critical part of our setup, demonstrating the safety precautions and techniques involved in navigating these challen

photos.caption: Lead Ice Climbing Course
photos.captionKii: Here’s another cool moment from our ice climbing trip! My friend, wearing his bright orange helmet and a rugged blue and yellow jacket, is intensely focused on the task at hand: carefully placing an ice screw into the formidable ice wall. You can see the snow clinging to his jacket and the surrounding branches, really highlighting the cold, pristine conditions we were climbing in. There's another piece of gear, possibly an ice axe, already anchored to the ice on the right, showing how well-equipped we were. It's these precise, deliberate actions that make ice climbing such an engaging and chal

photos.caption: Lead Ice Climbing Course
photos.captionKii: Here’s another shot from our ice climbing adventure, showing us gathered and focused on some crucial technical aspects. The person in the red helmet in the center is demonstrating or explaining something important with the ropes and gear, possibly about setting up our next climb or a safety procedure. I'm in the foreground with my orange helmet and blue jacket, paying close attention, along with our other friends to the right, all bundled up in our winter climbing gear. The snow-covered ground, trees, and hints of ice in the background really capture the challenging yet exhilarating environmen

photos.caption: Lead Ice Climbing Course
photos.captionKii: Another awesome moment from our ice climbing expedition! Here’s my friend in the foreground, looking good in his dark hooded jacket and a white helmet, smiling as he points something out on the ice. He’s got his ice axes ready and attached to his gear, firmly planted in the impressive ice wall we're tackling. I'm behind him on the right, geared up with my red helmet, watching the action and enjoying the intense focus and exhilaration of being out here in this beautiful, snowy environment. It's truly incredible to be pushing our limits against these frozen formations with good company.

photos.caption: Lead Ice Climbing Course
photos.captionKii: What an exhilarating day out on the ice! Here I am, geared up for some serious ice climbing with a friend. I'm on the right, wearing my red helmet and a warm dark jacket, focused on the ropes and the climb ahead, while my friend on the left is fully equipped with his white helmet, dark jacket, and crampons, ready to tackle that impressive ice wall. We've got all our climbing gear, including ice axes and harnesses, visible and ready for action. The whole environment is blanketed in fresh snow, making for a truly stunning, albeit challenging, winter adventure.

photos.caption: Ski St-Adolphe d'Howard — Vraiment l'hivers c'est la plus belle saison avec les sapin charges de neige.
photos.captionKii: What a fantastic day for some cross-country skiing! Here we are, making our way through a beautiful, snow-covered forest. The trees are absolutely dusted with fresh snow, making everything look so peaceful and pristine. You can see me in the foreground, gliding along with my skis and poles, while a few of my friends are further back on the trail, enjoying the fresh air and the serene winter landscape just as much. It's moments like these, surrounded by nature and good company, that truly make our mountain adventures so memorable.

photos.caption: from-campo-el-salto
photos.captionKii: What an incredible view from our camp! This shot perfectly captures the scale of the mountains we're exploring. Here, you can see our colorful tents pitched on a rocky patch right by the expansive snowfield, with some of my friends gathered nearby, dwarfed by the towering peaks. The sky was just beginning to light up with the sun peeking over the distant ridge, illuminating the clouds and casting a beautiful glow over the snow-covered slopes. It truly felt like we were at the top of the world, surrounded by such raw, majestic beauty. These are the moments that make all the effort of a mountain

photos.caption: Vallecito [Cordon del Plata] — This is where I'm heading for the next week, up that valley (Las Vegas) on the left, up to "El salto de agua campo" and hopfully up Cerro Vallecito, the whitest peak.
photos.captionKii: What an absolutely breathtaking panoramic view of the mountains! The sky was such a brilliant, clear blue, really making those rugged peaks stand out. You can see how varied the landscape is here, with some mountains still holding onto patches of snow in the distance, while closer to us, the terrain is more rocky and earthy, painted in these incredible reds and browns. And look, there are even a couple of wild horses grazing on that sparse, rocky hillside in the mid-ground. It just shows how untamed and expansive this region truly is. Moments like these, taking in such a grand scene, are just

photos.caption: Hike MtNoir / Grise — Deb et Michel revant de ski eux aussi!
photos.captionKii: What a gorgeous day for a winter trek! Here are two of my friends, all geared up in their warm jackets, hats, and sunglasses, making great progress through the deep snow. One of them is leaning into it, leaving a clear track, while the other is using ski poles, both looking focused and determined. The sun was really shining, making the snow sparkle and casting those long shadows behind them as we navigated through the peaceful, snow-covered landscape with its mix of evergreen and bare trees. It was another invigorating day out in the wilderness, full of fresh air and good company on our advent

photos.caption: Crise de rire
photos.captionKii: This photo perfectly captures the spirit of our winter trek! Here are two of my friends, all bundled up in their winter gear with their hats and gloves, absolutely beaming and laughing as we made our way through the snowy forest. The sun was really shining down, creating bright patches on the snow, even with all those fallen trees around us. It was such a great feeling to be out there with them, surrounded by the quiet beauty of the snow-covered woods, using our trekking poles to navigate the terrain. Their joy is just infectious, and it reminds me of the wonderful camaraderie we shared on thi

photos.caption: Tout sourir — Agu, Viv et Dave semblent tout sourir par cette journee de rando relax a Weir sous le soleil... il me faut retourner la pour grimper en ete, ca semble plutot impressionnant.
photos.captionKii: What a great shot of our crew enjoying the crisp, clear winter day! Here we are, all bundled up in our warm jackets and hats, smiling wide against the stunning backdrop of the snowy mountains. It was such a fantastic feeling to be out there with good company, navigating the snow-covered terrain and taking in that incredible view stretching out behind us. You can really feel the camaraderie and the joy of exploring this beautiful wilderness together. Moments like these, surrounded by friends and nature's grandeur, are what these adventures are all about.

photos.caption: Hike in weir — Mon premier WE de plein air apres le retour d'Argentine fut mieux que prevu... avec seulement quelques cm de neige le ski de fond etait impossible. Mais le soleil rendait la rando super agreable.
photos.captionKii: What a feeling to be out here! I remember trekking through this snowy landscape, with the sun bright in the sky, casting those long shadows behind us. It captures that moment perfectly—one of us with arms wide, just soaking in the vastness of it all, surrounded by those hardy evergreen and bare trees, with the distant mountains hazy on the horizon. The air was crisp and clear, and every step felt like we were really in the heart of nature, experiencing the quiet beauty of winter. It was such a memorable part of our adventure.

photos.caption: Speedy clouds
photos.captionKii: This panoramic shot truly captures the overwhelming scale and beauty of the mountains we were exploring. The deep, brilliant blue sky, scattered with those bright white clouds, really makes the dark, jagged peaks stand out. You can see how rugged and imposing they are, with patches of snow clinging to the upper slopes and crevices. Looking up at these giants, I felt completely immersed in the raw, untamed power of nature, a moment that perfectly encapsulates the grandeur of this adventure.

photos.caption: Speedy clouds
photos.captionKii: This shot truly captures the breathtaking expanse of the mountains we were amidst. The sky was an unbelievable deep blue, dotted with those perfect white clouds, making the snow-capped peaks stand out even more dramatically. You can really feel the immense scale of these rugged giants, with their dark rock faces emerging from the vast snowfields and glaciers. Every turn presented a view that just took my breath away, and moments like this were etched into my memory – pure, untamed nature at its finest.

photos.caption: Dead end
photos.captionKii: Wow, this view! It perfectly captures the immense scale and raw beauty of the mountains we were exploring. The deep blue sky provides such a striking contrast to the rugged, snow-capped peaks that stretch across the horizon. You can really see the harsh, rocky terrain, with scree slopes and massive boulders leading into what looks like a glacial valley or cirque in the center. The sheer geological drama of this place, with those deep shadows and powerful rock formations, was truly humbling. Every turn presented another breathtaking vista like this, reminding me of the incredible power of natur

photos.caption: Horcone glacier — The Horcone glacier leading up to Aconcagua south face it quite rough. Fortunately it is partly coverted with scree and it's possible to walk on flat ground next to it.
photos.captionKii: This panoramic shot truly captures the breathtaking grandeur of the mountains we were exploring. The sheer scale is incredible, with that massive snow-capped peak dominating the left side, and a visible glacier flowing down its base. To the right, the rugged, reddish-brown peaks stretch out under a clear blue sky. I can see a lone trekker, tiny against the vastness, making their way across the rough terrain, which really puts into perspective how immense and wild this landscape is. It was moments like these that made the entire expedition so awe-inspiring.

photos.caption: Horcone glacier valley — Going up the horcone valley to Plaza Francia give you a very good idea of what are andes trek are like. Mostly easy ground on firm scree, slow climbing slope, very long and large valleys with very steep rock/scree w
photos.captionKii: Wow, what an incredible panoramic shot from our mountain expedition! The sheer scale of these rugged peaks is just breathtaking, especially with the clear blue sky overhead. You can see the vast, rocky terrain we were traversing, a mix of scree and larger boulders, stretching far into the distance. Those majestic, snow-capped mountains in the background stand tall, looking almost untouched. It really puts into perspective how small we are out here. I remember seeing a few fellow trekkers making their way across that reddish-brown path in the middle ground, underscoring the solitude and challen

photos.caption: Confluencia by morning — Sunrise are very specatular in the area... but is there anything not amazing up there? It must be the fatigue and the higer (not that high) altitude that put "pink glasses" on my brain.
photos.captionKii: I was completely awestruck by this incredible panoramic view of the mountains, likely captured during either an early morning ascent or a serene late afternoon. The light is just spectacular, casting long shadows across the foreground, while the higher peaks on the right are bathed in a warm, golden glow, highlighting their rugged, snow-capped grandeur against the deep blue sky. Down below, nestled in the shadows, I can see our campsite with a line of tents and a small structure, which really emphasizes the scale of these magnificent mountains. It was such a powerful moment, feeling so small y

photos.caption: Reaction de mi suegra
photos.captionKii: I love this shot of this wonderful woman, her expression is absolutely priceless! She's got such a lively, wide-eyed look, almost as if she's just been surprised or is bursting with excitement, and that big smile is infectious. She's wearing a vibrant pink top and a lovely light purple beaded necklace, which really complements her warm complexion. The background, with its reflections, gives a hint of an indoor setting, and I can faintly see 'Canada' in the glass behind her, which brings back memories of our time there. It's a truly joyful and spirited moment captured!

photos.caption: Mendoza == Bodega... hic!
photos.captionKii: I loved stumbling upon this historical scene! Here's an old wooden wagon, likely used for transporting wine barrels, standing proudly in front of a rustic, pinkish-beige adobe building. The large, weathered barrels on the wagon give it such an authentic, aged feel. Above the entrance, a sign reads 'SALA COCHES Y CARROS,' meaning 'Room for Coaches and Carts,' confirming its historical purpose. On either side of the doorway, signs for 'Viña San Felipe' suggest we were visiting a beautiful old vineyard. It felt like stepping back in time, imagining the wine-making process from days gone by. There

photos.caption: Family dinner... Lovely distraction while waiting for my lift
photos.captionKii: This tranquil scene captures three horses, two adults and a foal, peacefully drinking from a calm body of water, their reflections softly visible on the surface. I remember being struck by the serenity of this moment, a beautiful encounter with the local wildlife. In the foreground, vibrant green ferns catch the sunlight, and two prominent tree trunks frame the left side of the shot. Beyond the water, a gently sloping hill covered in low, green vegetation stretches upwards, hinting at the vastness of the Patagonian landscape around us. It was a lovely, quiet observation during our journey.

photos.caption: Frozen
photos.captionKii: This shot perfectly captures the feeling of being high up in the mountains, surrounded by nothing but incredible natural beauty. I remember standing here, fully geared up in my red jacket, hood pulled tight against the wind, and my trekking poles planted firmly, feeling the crisp, cold air. The sun was shining, but you can see the snow-covered peaks and valleys stretching out endlessly behind me, highlighting the vastness of this Patagonian landscape. It was a truly exhilarating moment, soaking in the panoramic view after a challenging climb.

photos.caption: The path up Cerro Vallecito was quite easy going on scree (not too loose)
photos.captionKii: This shot truly captures the challenge and scale of our ascent. I remember looking up at this incredibly steep, rugged mountain slope, almost entirely covered in dark, loose scree. You can clearly see the narrow, winding path we were following, snaking its way relentlessly towards the summit. Up near the peak, there's even a persistent patch of snow and ice clinging to the dark rock, a testament to the high altitude and cold conditions. The sky overhead was a mix of brilliant blue and dramatic white clouds, adding to the wild beauty of this Patagonian landscape. Every step on that path felt li

photos.caption: Aube sur le camp
photos.captionKii: This dawn captured the serene beauty after a cold night at camp. I remember waking up to this incredible sky, watching the colors slowly paint the horizon from deep purples to fiery oranges and reds, just before the sun peeked over the distant peaks. The silhouette of the massive mountain against that vibrant sky was breathtaking, and below, you can see the faint outlines of our dome-shaped shelter and tents, still tucked into the rugged, dark terrain. There's even a wisp of cloud floating in the valley, reminding me how high we were. It was a moment of quiet reflection, preparing for another

photos.caption: Why weather forecasting is hard in the mountains — Ever wanted a proof that weather in the mountains change from one valley to the other, from one side of a ridge to the other? There you have. Unfortunately my tent was on the wrong side :-(
photos.captionKii: This shot truly captures the intense, dynamic weather we encountered in Patagonia. I remember looking towards this imposing, dark mountain peak, its rocky face stark against the bright blue sky on the left. But what really struck me was the way the clouds seemed to be pouring over its ridge on the right, creating this incredible, almost mystical curtain as they enveloped the summit. In the foreground, the ground is all rugged scree and scattered patches of snow, clearly showing how high up we were. And beyond, the vast expanse of the Andes just kept unfolding, with more snow-dusted peaks stret

photos.caption: Cerro Aconcagua from Cerro El Plata slopes [just a bit above el Portesuelo]
photos.captionKii: This panoramic view truly takes my breath away. I remember standing there, looking out over this immense expanse of the Patagonian landscape. In the foreground, you can see the rugged scree and patches of snow, remnants of our climb. Beyond that, the dramatic, dark mountain ridges rise sharply, covered in a beautiful quilt of snow. And in the far distance, under that impossibly clear blue sky, there's that magnificent, massive snow-capped peak standing guard over everything. It was one of those moments where the sheer scale and beauty of the Andes just completely overwhelmed me, making all the

photos.caption: More wind shaped clouds
photos.captionKii: This shot truly encapsulates the raw majesty of Patagonia. I remember standing there, looking up at this formidable snow-capped peak, its dark, rocky face sharply defined against the brilliant blue sky on one side. On the other, dramatic, swirling clouds seemed to be pouring over the summit, creating an almost mystical effect as they clung to the jagged edges. The foreground, with its loose scree, reminds me of the challenging ascent and the incredible altitude we reached to witness such breathtaking views. It's moments like these that make the entire journey unforgettable.

photos.caption: Cloud Trail on cerro Vallecito — Among all the things I saw, the most impressive ones were clouds. Especially over the Portesuelo and cerro Vallecito. The valley from where I came up was filled of clouds, going up.  The one west of it was c
photos.captionKii: This view truly captures the raw power of the Patagonian landscape. I remember looking up at this incredible snow-capped peak, shrouded dramatically in swirling clouds that seemed to cling to its jagged edges. The stark contrast between the deep blue sky, the brilliant white snow, and the dark, rocky scree slope in the foreground was just breathtaking. You can almost feel the cold wind whipping around the summit, making the clouds dance around the mountain. It was another moment that reminded me of the wild, untamed beauty of this region.

photos.caption:
Lava? — Not sure if these mountains were once volcano. There are lots of nicely colored rocks, some yellow (sulfur?) and these dark, burned like, rocks.
If someone knows, please leave a comment to explain. Thanks!
photos.captionKii: This shot really highlights the incredible contrasts we encountered in Patagonia. I remember trekking along this incredibly steep mountainside, where the dark, jagged scree and rocky terrain met a sharp, pristine line of bright white snow. The blue sky above was so vivid, making the whole scene even more dramatic. You can just make out a faint path winding through the darker scree, reminding me of the challenging routes we navigated through this wild landscape.

photos.caption: Burning me — The sun, the wind and the cold were quite hard on the skin... better be properly wraped!
photos.captionKii: I vividly remember this moment of our trek through Patagonia. My companion, fully kitted out in her bright red jacket, sunglasses, and a hat, stands confidently with her trekking poles, navigating the rough, rocky path. Behind her, the landscape is simply immense – jagged, dark mountains rise dramatically, their snowy peaks partially shrouded in those magnificent, swirling clouds. Above, patches of intense blue sky cut through, making the whole scene feel so grand and challenging. It really captures the spirit of adventure and the breathtaking beauty we found in this wild place.

photos.caption: Snow path — There was not much snow around there. Despite the fact they call most snow patch galciers, it's not you usual one with ice, crevasses and serac. It's more like a gentle slope on "neve".
photos.captionKii: This shot really captures the essence of our trek through Patagonia. I remember looking up at this incredibly steep, snow-covered slope, with those deep blue skies overhead. The way the dark, rugged rock protrudes through the snow on the upper parts of the mountain creates such a striking contrast. You can even see the faint tracks in the fresh snow, a testament to our path through this challenging and magnificent landscape. It was a clear, crisp day, and the sunlight really made the snow sparkle against the deep blue.

photos.caption: La Hoyada — La Hoyada is the 1st camp above Campo el salto de Agua. It is just a bit less than 500m above and apparently the air pressure varies a lot (=headache). Combine this with no water and just a 45-60min hike to get up there... No wo
photos.captionKii: This shot really captures the scale of our high-altitude camp in Patagonia. I remember standing in this vast, snow-covered valley, looking up at those immense, rugged mountains towering above, their slopes a mix of dark rock and bright white snow. The sky was an incredible, clear blue with just a few wispy clouds, a perfect day for taking in the grandeur. You can even spot our small yellow tents nestled against the dark, rocky hill in the midground, looking tiny against the immense backdrop of snow and rock. There are faint tracks in the snow leading away, reminders of our trek through this tr

photos.caption: Coulds invasion — Was impressive to see the clouds invading this valley just an hour after I passed there. And a few hours later for the rest of the day the valley was filled with clouds up to the higest pass, el Portesuelo at about 5000m.
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing these incredible clouds swirling and rising through the mountain pass, almost like they were being breathed into existence by the mountains themselves. The light was hitting them just right, making them billow and glow against the deep blue sky, which still had a few wispy clouds stretching across it. Below, the dark, rugged slopes were still covered in patches of bright snow, creating such a stark and beautiful contrast. It felt so raw and powerful, witnessing nature's artistry unfold in this vast Patagonian landscape.

photos.caption: Sunrise on this perfecly clear valley above the clouds... see next pic about 8min later...
photos.captionKii: This view truly encapsulates the rugged beauty we encountered on our Patagonian journey. I remember looking out at these vast, snow-dusted mountains, their dark, rocky slopes contrasting so dramatically with the patches of bright white snow scattered across the foreground. The deep valley stretches out before us, leading the eye towards the distant horizon where the sky is a brilliant blue, topped with those long, wispy clouds that signal such high altitude. The morning or late afternoon light creates such stark shadows and highlights, making every peak and crevice stand out, really emphasizin

photos.caption: Above the clouds
photos.captionKii: This really captures the essence of being high up in the mountains, especially with that incredible cloud inversion below! I remember looking out at this vast, rugged landscape, where the dark, rocky slopes, streaked with patches of bright snow, contrasted so beautifully with the intense blue sky. You can even see some wisps of cloud lifting slightly from the main cloud layer. It truly felt like standing on top of the world, looking down at an endless sea of clouds, with the sun casting that clear, sharp light across everything. Such a powerful and serene moment, completely immersed in the raw

photos.caption: Ombre chinoise?
photos.captionKii: I remember this moment so vividly—trekking through the bright, sunlit snow, and looking down to see my own shadow stretching out before me, complete with my backpack and what looks like a trekking pole. The snow itself was so pristine and undulating, creating these beautiful, soft shadows and highlights that really caught the light. It's funny how a simple shadow can encapsulate the feeling of the journey, reminding me of being high up in the mountains, under a clear sky, just pushing forward through that incredible Patagonian landscape.

photos.caption: Magnificent sunrise from el campo salto de agua
photos.captionKii: Wow, this shot perfectly captures one of those absolutely glorious moments high up in Patagonia. I remember the sun was just incredibly intense, bursting over the horizon and casting those incredible long rays, creating such a dramatic lens flare across the deep blue sky. Below us, it was an endless sea of fluffy white clouds, stretching out as far as I could see – definitely felt like we were above the world. In the foreground, the dark, rugged terrain, probably volcanic rock or scree, with just a few patches of snow, added to that raw, untamed feeling of being so high up. It was truly breath

photos.caption: Magnificent sunrise from el campo salto de agua — Despite the cold and fatigue, it was hard not to get up a bit before 6AM to see this. I just wish I was a bit less lazy so that I could have been up on the summit at that time. :-/
photos.captionKii: What an incredible sight to wake up to, or perhaps to experience at sunset, high up in the mountains! I remember this distinct camp with the large, cylindrical structure that had 'kalu' on it, alongside our smaller individual tents. The ground was so rocky and rugged, typical of these high altitudes. The sun was just bursting over the horizon, casting this intense golden light and creating dramatic rays across the sky. Below us, or perhaps just beyond the ridge, were these magnificent, rolling clouds, glowing warmly from the sun. It really emphasizes how high up we were, looking down at the cl

photos.caption: Clouds in fire
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures one of those breathtaking atmospheric moments high up in Patagonia. I remember seeing these incredible, fluffy white clouds lit by the low sun, stretching across the horizon like a soft, luminous band. Below them, the entire landscape was enveloped in a deep, ethereal blue haze, making the distant mountains barely visible and creating a profound sense of depth and mystery. In the foreground, the dark silhouette of a mountainside framed the scene, adding to the dramatic contrast between the clear sky above the clouds and the muted world below. It felt like standing at

photos.caption: Wind sheild... for my stove
photos.captionKii: I remember this moment clearly from our Patagonian journey, preparing a meal amidst the stark, rocky landscape. Here, you can see our cooking pot, nestled securely within a makeshift wind guard made from a silvery metal sheet, with a spoon resting inside the yellowish contents. It's surrounded by these huge, dark grey boulders, which we often used as natural shelters. Nearby, our red fuel bottle, easily recognizable with its black zigzag design, powered the stove. It was such a fundamental part of our daily routine out there – finding a sheltered spot, setting up camp, and cooking a warm meal

photos.caption: Base Camp at the limit of clouds
photos.captionKii: This panoramic shot truly captures the scale and dramatic setting of our high-altitude camp during the Patagonian journey. I remember looking down and seeing our array of tents, both the larger communal ones with the 'AKAU' logo and our smaller individual shelters, all nestled precariously on the rugged, rocky terrain. The light and shadows, accentuated by the black and white, make the scene feel incredibly stark and grand. photos.caption: Cerro Rincon in the sunset
photos.captionKii: This shot perfectly captures the raw, formidable beauty of the Patagonian mountains. I remember looking up at this magnificent, snow-covered peak, rising dramatically from a dark, rugged base of rocks and scree. The contrast between the stark white snow and the deep, rich tones of the earth was just incredible. Above, the sky was a vibrant blue, but those characteristic Patagonian clouds were already gathering, some clinging right to the summit, adding so much depth and drama to the scene. It was moments like these, feeling so small yet completely surrounded by such immense natural power, that

photos.caption: Looking down from Campo el salto de agua
photos.captionKii: This sweeping panorama really brings back the incredible scale of the Patagonian landscape. I remember standing high up, looking across these vast, rocky mountainsides with their scattered patches of snow, feeling so small amidst it all. The foreground is rugged and strewn with dark rocks, giving way to these magnificent, sun-drenched slopes. In the far distance, I could just make out the hazy outlines of a sprawling valley and what looked like a large lake or a wide river, hinting at the immense distances we were covering. Above, the sky was a brilliant blue with those characteristic dramatic

photos.caption: Fast moving clouds up the valley
photos.captionKii: This shot truly captures the dramatic weather we experienced during our Patagonian journey. I remember looking up at these incredible, dark grey mountain slopes, crisscrossed with streaks of pristine white snow. The sky was a brilliant blue, but these magnificent, billowing white clouds were rolling in, creating such a dynamic and powerful scene. Down in the lower left, you can just make out a few of us, small figures against the vastness, trekking across the rocky terrain. It really gives a sense of the scale and the rugged beauty of the environment we were exploring, with the clouds constant

photos.caption: Sunset over el Portesuelo (pass between Cerro Plata y Cerro Vallecito)
photos.captionKii: This shot truly captures the breathtaking grandeur of the Patagonian mountains during our journey. I remember looking up at this incredible scene: vast, snow-covered slopes leading up to dark, formidable peaks under a dramatic sky. The clouds were magnificent, with patches of bright blue showing through, and the sun was just breaking through a haze on the right, casting a brilliant light over everything. It felt so high and wild, with rocks in the immediate foreground hinting at the rugged terrain we were traversing. Moments like these, standing amidst such raw, powerful nature, were truly unf

photos.caption: Little hobbo ;o)
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this resilient dog during our Patagonian journey, standing so calmly amidst the rocky, scree-covered terrain. Its fur, a mix of light brown and dark, blended perfectly with the natural tones of the landscape, and it wore a simple brown collar. It looks like it's squinting a bit, maybe from the bright light or just mid-blink. In the background, the rugged mountains with their patches of snow against the cloudy sky really emphasize the remote and wild environment we were exploring. It was wonderful to encounter such a creature thriving in this harsh, beautiful landscape.

photos.caption: Clouds invading Campo el salto de agua
photos.captionKii: This vast shot really takes me back to the scale of our Patagonian journey. I remember standing amidst these huge, dark grey boulders that formed the rugged, scree-covered foreground of our high-altitude camp. You can spot a few of our tents, including a bright red one and a yellow one, nestled into the rocky landscape, and one of our fellow trekkers in a yellow jacket is visible in the distance. The towering, dramatic mountains with their patches of snow against the cloudy sky, occasionally revealing a sliver of blue, made the whole scene feel incredibly wild and majestic. It was a constant r

photos.caption: Water pit... just between the camp and the "toilet" zone!!! Yuck!
photos.captionKii: This photo is a small but essential detail from our Patagonian adventure. I remember needing to gather fresh water, and this blue plastic cup served perfectly for scooping from the pristine, ice-cold stream fed by melting snow. The water carved a path through the snow and rocks, revealing the rugged ground underneath. It really highlights the simple, yet vital, aspects of our daily life while trekking through such a remote and beautiful high-altitude landscape.

photos.caption: Was a bit foggy at the camp... for the whole week!
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures the challenging weather conditions we faced during our Patagonian journey. I remember looking out from a vantage point, probably near our camp, and seeing the entire landscape shrouded in an incredibly thick, heavy fog. The rocky, dark reddish-brown terrain, almost like a moonscape, emerged only vaguely from the mist, with just a patch of snow visible on the rugged ground. Up on the distant ridge, I could barely make out the shapes of our tents, including a yellow one, and possibly some of our companions or packed animals, all battling the limited visibility. It was a

photos.caption: Cutty... feels good without the load!
photos.captionKii: This photo brings back memories of one of our essential companions on the Patagonian journey. I remember seeing this beautiful brown mule, fully saddled and packed with gear, standing so patiently against the misty, overcast sky. The ropes and tack are clearly visible, showing how well-equipped it was for our expedition through the rugged terrain. In the background, the rocky, scree-covered ground and patches of snow really emphasize the high-altitude environment we were navigating. I also spotted another packed animal further down the slope, almost blending into the fog. These animals were ab

photos.caption: Camp guardian
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures a peaceful moment amidst our high-altitude Patagonian journey. I remember seeing this incredibly fluffy, large dog, likely a St. Bernard or similar mountain breed, just lounging comfortably on the rocky, scree-covered ground. In the background, our colorful dome tents – a vibrant blue one nestled near a patch of snow and an orange one further back, along with another light blue tent – are pitched amongst the boulders. The misty clouds roll over the distant mountains, emphasizing the remote and wild feel of this incredible landscape. It really highlights the diverse co

photos.caption: It's bear, no a yeti... no a... ?!dog!?
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures a peaceful moment amidst our rugged Patagonian journey. I remember seeing this incredibly fluffy, large dog, possibly a St. Bernard or similar mountain breed, just lounging comfortably on the rocky ground near our camp. In the background, our yellow dome tent is pitched amongst the boulders, and further up the rise, our sturdy horses, loaded with gear, stand patiently. It really highlights the diverse companions we had on this expedition, from fellow adventurers to these wonderful animals, all sharing the same wild, untamed landscape under that vast, overcast sky.

photos.caption: Campo el salto de agua... first look
photos.captionKii: This photo truly brings me back to setting up camp in the midst of our Patagonian journey. I remember seeing all these tents, some smaller personal ones in vibrant oranges and yellows scattered across the rocky, rugged ground, alongside those larger white base camp shelters or communal tents in the background. The air was thick with mist and clouds, creating this incredible, almost surreal atmosphere, and you can still see patches of snow clinging to the upper slopes. We had all our gear and bags laid out, and a few people are visible near the bigger tents, bundled up against the chill. It rea

photos.caption: Water at last! El Salto de Agua... just below camp
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures the raw, rugged beauty of the Patagonian high country. I remember seeing this sturdy horse, saddled up, making its way carefully across the steep, rocky scree slope. Just to the left, there's a significant patch of snow, probably a remnant glacier or a high-altitude snowfield, carving a path down the mountainside, contrasting sharply with the dark, jagged rocks. The sky looks a bit overcast, emphasizing the remote and wild feel of this incredible landscape. It's moments like these, seeing how life adapts and moves through such challenging terrain, that really define t

photos.caption: Going up... like a mule
photos.captionKii: This shot really brings back the feeling of pushing through the high-altitude Patagonian mountains. I remember one of us, bundled up in a bright red jacket, a cap, and sunglasses, paused here, using trekking poles for support on the incredibly rocky and uneven terrain. The ground was just a mix of loose stones and boulders, making every step a deliberate effort. In the background, the vast, rugged slopes stretched out, looking quite barren with hints of distant peaks under a cloudy sky. Moments like these, where you're fully immersed in the raw, untamed wilderness, truly define the adventure.

photos.caption: Heavy pack
photos.captionKii: This shot truly brings back the feeling of pushing through the rugged Patagonian terrain. I remember seeing these sturdy pack mules, loaded with our yellow dry bags and other gear, making their way up the steep, rocky slope. Two riders are guiding them, navigating the loose stones and rough path. The scale of the mountainside is immense, a barren, rocky expanse under what feels like a vast, open sky, emphasizing just how remote and challenging our journey was. It really captures the essence of our expedition, relying on these incredible animals to carry our essentials through such an untamed l

photos.caption: Mule train
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures the challenging yet incredibly rewarding aspect of our Patagonian journey. I remember seeing our group, or perhaps another small expedition, making their way up these steep, rocky mountain slopes on horseback, or with mules carrying their gear. The terrain was rugged, covered in large boulders and sparse vegetation, giving a real sense of the remote wilderness. In the background, the majestic mountain peak is partly shrouded in dramatic clouds, with hints of snow further up, underscoring the high altitude and the epic scale of our adventure. Moments like these, pushin

photos.caption: Mad cow — This cow looks really weird. But what's more is that it was acting weird... or simply feeling lonely. It's been following me for quite a while, until I reach the "no more grass" zone. Do I look like I cow? [Please do not answer] ;
photos.captionKii: Another peaceful moment from our Patagonian journey, capturing this beautiful brown and white cow grazing quietly on a vast, gently sloping hillside. The ground is a mix of green grass and scattered rocks, giving a real sense of the rugged, natural environment. In the background, the majestic mountains rise, some with snowy patches, under a striking blue sky dotted with fluffy white clouds, reminding me again of the immense scale and untamed beauty of this incredible landscape.

photos.caption: Troupeau perdu?
photos.captionKii: This shot brings back the immense feeling of being out in the wild Patagonian landscapes. I remember seeing these cows, with their distinct brown and white markings, grazing so peacefully in the wide-open grassy valley. The land stretched out endlessly before the dramatic, reddish-brown mountains, some still capped with snow, all under a vast blue sky dotted with a few clouds. It really emphasizes the scale of our journey, and how life here just unfolds against such an epic, untamed backdrop.

photos.caption: Las Vegas... not your usual casino place — This is the last bit of water in the valley. I walked the next 1000 vertical meter w/o seing any other source of water. I was a bit concerned, going ther for a week without enough fuel to melt snow
photos.captionKii: This photo truly captures the raw beauty of our Patagonian journey. I remember standing there, taking in this incredible view of a crystal-clear stream bubbling its way through a vibrant green, rocky valley. The water was so pure, reflecting the clear blue sky. In the background, the majestic mountains rise, some still holding onto their snow-capped peaks under a bright blue sky dotted with scattered white clouds. The dirt path on the right side hints at the trails we explored, making this another perfect memory of the vast, untamed wilderness we were so fortunate to experience.

photos.caption: Poulain curieux — This horse was so curious (or short sighted) that he came very close to me... but I'm an idot so I made a rapid move to pet him which scared him away.
photos.captionKii: This shot really captured a beautiful moment from our Patagonian journey. I remember spotting this adorable light brown foal, standing so calmly on the rocky, grassy terrain. Its striking white blaze on its face really stood out, making it quite distinctive. The background, with those majestic reddish-brown and tan mountains under a clear blue sky, just reinforced the wild, rugged beauty of where we were. It was a wonderful encounter, a true highlight of seeing the local wildlife thriving in this incredible, untamed landscape.

photos.caption: Poulain curieux
photos.captionKii: This shot captures another incredible vista from our Patagonian adventure. I remember seeing this beautiful light brown horse with its distinctive white blaze standing calmly on the reddish, rocky terrain, seemingly unfazed by its majestic surroundings. The vastness of the landscape is truly humbling, with rolling green-brown hills leading up to the towering snow-capped peaks of the Andes under that perfectly clear, vibrant blue sky. It's moments like these, encountering the local wildlife against such an epic backdrop, that truly defined the wild beauty of our journey, a perfect memory of the

photos.caption: Paturage — Amazing how horses are left loose... well almost lose since one of them had the front legs tied together so he couldn't run away.
photos.captionKii: This photo beautifully captures the vast, untamed essence of Patagonia. I remember standing there, taking in the incredible scale of the landscape. A lone brown horse is peacefully grazing in the foreground, its head down, adding a sense of quiet solitude to the scene. Behind it, a small stream carves its way down the gently sloping, rocky green hills, eventually disappearing into the distance. Dominating the background are the majestic, snow-capped peaks of the Andes, reaching towards a perfectly clear blue sky. It was a moment of pure serenity, a perfect encapsulation of the wild beauty we w

photos.caption: Asado — Asado is the Sunday tradition in Argentina. Even the most remote places are making one and lots of people a gathering to share it. Lovely country where people meet and share.
photos.captionKii: This photo really takes me back to the Patagonian journey, capturing a hearty outdoor meal. I remember seeing one of our companions expertly tending to a massive grill setup, laden with a huge spread of meats and an array of different sausages. The smoke rising from the coals and the impressive quantity of food made it clear this was going to be a substantial and delicious meal after our adventures. In the background, the familiar yellow mountain-style buildings and the rugged, rocky terrain reinforced that we were still deep in the beautiful, wild landscape of Patagonia, enjoying a well-deser

photos.caption: Local Mountaineer — The good thing about huts and base camps is that you meet lots of like minded people. Especially just before the peak trekking season I was able to meet lots of locals with plenty of stories to tell, plenty of info to sh
photos.captionKii: This photo captures a warm moment with a man I encountered during our Patagonian journey. He's sitting at a rustic wooden table, looking directly at the camera with a friendly gaze. He's wearing a cool black baseball cap with an interesting emblem, a vibrant red and black zip-up jacket over a teal shirt, and a unique beaded necklace. The dimly lit, cozy wooden interior, with its visible wooden walls and benches in the background, feels very much like the lodges or cabins we've been staying in. In front of him, I can see a plastic bag containing what looks like 'Fango' bread or pastries, probab

photos.caption: Relaxing Cozy — After the few days I spent in the aconcagua parc I had to stop to do some blister resting. The hut at Ski y Montana was cold but nice enough with warm tea, hot showers and a real "colchon"!
photos.captionKii: This photo captures a cozy moment of rest indoors during our Patagonian journey. I remember seeing one of my companions, bundled up in his bright green puffer jacket and black beanie, relaxing on what looks like a bunk bed in a rustic wooden cabin. He's looking towards me with a relaxed smile, his head propped on his hand. The warm glow of the orange lantern hanging from the wooden ceiling beams really added to the comfortable, almost rustic atmosphere. It was a well-deserved break after a day of exploring, with his water bottle close by, ready for our next adventure.

photos.caption: The 3rd (baby) range — Funny how things look small from high above (~3000m). Apparently the area has 3 main mountain range. The high/border one where Aconcagua stands. Cordon del Plata where I sit to shoot that pic and this 3rd one.
photos.captionKii: This panoramic shot truly captures the immense scale of the Patagonian landscape that we've been exploring. I remember looking out at these seemingly endless rolling mountains and deep valleys, bathed in the soft, dramatic light of what I think was either dawn or dusk. The interplay of light and shadow on the hillsides creates such a beautiful texture, with some slopes appearing dark and rugged in the foreground, and others in the distance glowing with a pale green. You can even spot faint dirt roads winding through the valley floor, hinting at the paths we’ve taken, and those tiny white struc

photos.caption: Modestie — Nice pic in the refurio. These guys did Aconcagua up and down in about 6h (going up in 4h30)... most people take a least a few days, if not weeks, to do it. If you feel you're a king because you climbed some high mountains... ple
photos.captionKii: This photo captures an intriguing poster I came across during our journey, commemorating the 'Aconcagua 1992' expedition. It proudly announces a 'WORLD RECORD at 7,035 m' achieved with the Military High Mountain Group. The main image features the majestic reddish slopes of Aconcagua, with a paraglider descending from near the summit, truly a sight to behold. Smaller inset photos show the expedition team in blue and red uniforms, climbers traversing snowy terrain with poles, and someone photographing a snowy vista. The text below details new records set on February 23rd, including rapid ascent

photos.caption: Sucio y sucio — sucio is the spanish word for dirty
photos.captionKii: This photo captures another lovely memory from our Patagonian journey, a relaxed moment where I saw one of my companions taking a break with a truly magnificent dog. He's smiling warmly at the camera while gently petting this huge, fluffy Saint Bernard-like dog, which looks so content. We were at what seems to be a sunny outdoor rest stop, with white tables and chairs in the background, and the rugged, rocky terrain stretching out behind us. It really highlights those wonderful, unexpected encounters that make a trip so special, even amidst all the adventure and exploration of the mountains.

photos.caption: Please pet me! — This dog was amazing. He was bumping his head and rubing his fur on my legs to get attention. It's a chance I was stinking as much as he did!!!
photos.captionKii: This photo captures a really sweet moment from our Patagonian adventure. Here, I saw one of my companions gently petting a friendly dog, whose head is resting comfortably on his arm. He's got such a warm smile, and you can tell there's a real connection with the dog, which has a mix of black, white, and brown fur. We were out on what looks like a sunny patio, with all those white tables and chairs spread out, probably a spot where we stopped for a break. The rugged, rocky mountain slopes in the background, topped with some green patches, definitely remind me that we were still deep in the Pata

photos.caption: Ski y Montana — Finally I got to Vallecito, the tiny remote ski center almost a abandoned. The welcome commettee was a bit sleepy...
photos.captionKii: This photo brings back memories of one of our rest stops during the Patagonian journey. We stumbled upon this charming, rustic yellow building with its distinct window frames, which I remember felt like a welcome sight after traversing the rugged landscapes. What really caught my eye was this incredibly fluffy, shaggy dog, completely sprawled out and fast asleep on the concrete patio. It looked so content and relaxed in the warm sun. You can even spot a white folding chair nearby, hinting at a place to sit and relax, perhaps grab a drink. It perfectly captured the quieter moments amidst our ad

photos.caption: Thanks a bunch — If it were not from this guy the second part of my trip would have started in a much less enjoyable way. I got lots of contradicting information on how to go from Puente del Inca to Vallecito/Cordon del Plata. Some saying t
photos.captionKii: This shot really captures another adventurous facet of our Patagonian journey. Here, I saw a man and a young boy, who was geared up in a chest protector, standing proudly beside an impressive mountain bike. It looked like they were either just about to hit the trails or had just finished an exhilarating ride down those rugged mountain slopes. The background, with its rocky, barren terrain and the imposing mountains under a cloudy sky, perfectly reflects the wild and untamed nature of this region. It was cool to see the local presence and the extreme sports that are part of the culture here, ad

photos.caption: Arieros
photos.captionKii: This shot really brings back memories of our journey through the incredible Patagonian terrain. Here, you can see one of our companions expertly riding a mule along a narrow, winding dirt path, with another pack mule, heavily loaded with our supplies, diligently following. We were deep in a valley, with a rocky riverbed tracing its way beside us, and the sheer, imposing slopes of the mountains rising high on both sides. The distant peaks still wore their caps of snow, reminding us of the immense scale of this wild landscape. This was definitely an adventurous way to cover ground and experience

photos.caption: Bleu et blanc sur bleu et blanc — Sun, white cloud and blue sky in the background and on the foreground.
photos.captionKii: This shot truly captures the dramatic mood and immense scale of Patagonia. I remember seeing this Argentinian flag, silhouetted against the bright, cloud-filled sky, with the imposing snow-capped mountains forming a dark, jagged backdrop. The way the sun broke through the clouds, backlighting everything, made the flag stand out as a proud symbol in that wild, untamed landscape. It was a powerful visual, embodying the spirit of our journey through this incredible region.

photos.caption: National flag...
photos.captionKii: This shot really captures the vastness and rugged beauty of the Patagonian landscape. I remember seeing this flag, which I believe is Argentina's, proudly standing against the backdrop of those immense mountains. Their jagged peaks and dramatic shadows from the setting or rising sun tell a story of ancient geological forces and the sheer scale of the environment we were exploring. The way the sunlight illuminates the upper ridges while the lower slopes are cast in deep shadow creates such a powerful contrast, emphasizing the untamed nature of this incredible region.

photos.caption: Got too much Oxygen? Play volleyball and drink beer! — Life at Confluencia camp is surprising. Some people are just lying down in the tent, drinking lots of water to ease headache and other atlitude related sickness... others are playing vo
photos.captionKii: After a day of intense trekking, it was such a welcome sight to get back to base camp and unwind a bit. I captured this shot of a few of us enjoying a lively game of volleyball, with the dramatic backdrop of those massive, snow-capped Patagonian mountains. You can clearly see the different tents making up our camp, and that yellow 'SERVICES' sign was always a relief, reminding us of the basic comforts available even out here in the wilderness. It was a great way to bond and relax after our explorations.

photos.caption: Looking down on the glacier. — Wow that's a long way... yeah a very long way to go down without water! Looks like 4 liters is not enough for this hike (something like 5-6h, of course it's not that long if you avoid the detour as I did!) in
photos.captionKii: Wow, this view really shows the massive scale of the Patagonian landscape! I captured this wide shot of a valley flanked by striking reddish-brown mountains, with a huge, textured glacier or ice field stretching across the center. You can see the distinct white ice contrasting with the darker debris, and in the distance, more towering snow-capped peaks emerge under that brilliant blue sky with fluffy clouds. It felt so immense and wild to be there, another incredible moment on our journey.

photos.caption: Avalancha! — A saw a fair amount of avalanche on the south face while going up the valley. I've been told that some people are climbing it. Is there any low avalanche risk sector or season? Or are these people just brain dead?
photos.captionKii: Wow, this shot captures such an intense moment in the mountains! I remember seeing these incredible glaciers and the powerful waterfalls cascading down the rugged rock faces. That plume of mist or snow dust rising from the slopes really emphasizes the dynamic, untamed nature of this Patagonian landscape. It's a vivid reminder of the sheer power and scale of the environment we were exploring.

photos.caption: Plaza Francia... Avalanche observatory — I finally got to Plaza Francia... but with a detour. Willing to get some height I did take a higher route above the main trail and ended up much further up in the valley until I give up looking for i
photos.captionKii: What an incredible landmark! I captured this shot of a rustic wooden signpost proudly marking 'Plaza Francia' against the truly magnificent backdrop of a massive, snow-capped mountain range. The sheer scale of these peaks, with their dramatic snowfields and jagged rocky slopes, is just awe-inspiring under that clear blue sky dotted with white clouds. The reddish-brown, rocky terrain in the foreground really highlights the rugged environment we're trekking through. It felt like such an accomplishment to reach this point and take in the grandeur of it all.

photos.caption: Speedy clouds over Aconcagua south face
photos.captionKii: What a stunning view! This shot perfectly captures the dramatic scale of the Patagonian landscape. On the left, a majestic, snow-capped mountain gleams under the sun, while on the right, a rugged, dark rocky slope rises sharply, partially shadowed. Above, the sky is an incredibly deep blue, dotted with a brilliant white cloud, making the whole scene feel even more vast and untamed. It truly highlights the raw, powerful beauty we encountered on our journey.

photos.caption: Funny face and south face — Hmmm looks like I was not very happy, not because I was looking away from Aconcagua south face. It's just that the camera was falling sideway  while taking the pics... thanks to photo editing tools who can make i
photos.captionKii: Standing proudly with two of my companions, we paused for a photo against the truly monumental backdrop of a massive, snow-capped Patagonian peak. The sky above us is a brilliant, clear blue, contrasting sharply with the reddish-brown rocky terrain that makes up the lower slopes and the ground beneath our feet. My friends are geared up, one in a red jacket and beanie, the other in a dark blue jacket and cap, both looking determined yet pleased to be amidst such grandeur. It's moments like these that truly capture the scale and raw beauty of our adventure here.

photos.caption: Rock "rainbow" [rockbow?] — I'm still amazed with the rock color pallet in these mountains, all shades of brown, red, yellow, gray, black and even white. Add the green vegetation (sparse) and the blues sky...
photos.captionKii: As we continued our journey through Patagonia, I was captivated by these incredibly sharp and dramatic rock formations along a mountain ridge. The way they jut out against the clear blue sky, with different shades of dark and light rock, really emphasized the rugged and ancient feel of this landscape. It felt like standing in a geological masterpiece, each peak telling a story of countless years of erosion and tectonic shifts. I just had to capture this close-up view of the raw power and beauty of these mountains.

photos.caption: Meet Senad — I met Senad on the bus from Mendoza. Very nice guy whose been living for 9 years in Argentina... I would not have been able to tell from his accent (I'm not Argentinian though). Unfortunately he had a hard time going up to Conf
photos.captionKii: Stopping for a moment of rest, one of my companions sits on a large rock, taking a break from our trek through the rugged Patagonian landscape. They've got their backpack on and are leaning on their trekking poles, perfectly framed by the vast, arid valley stretching out behind them. The sun is bright, casting a long shadow, and the clear blue sky overhead highlights the impressive snow-capped mountains in the distance. This really captures the essence of our journey, traversing these incredible, untamed wildernesses.

photos.caption: Paturage — Confluencia doesn't look that dry from here. Don't know how these plants survive.
photos.captionKii: Looking across this expansive valley, I'm struck by the sheer scale of the Patagonian landscape. The sun is just starting to peek over the rugged mountains to the left, casting a beautiful flare and illuminating the sparse, grassy terrain and scattered rocks. In the distance, along the base of the mountain on the right, our line of tents forms a familiar sight – another one of our camps, offering a welcome resting point in this vast wilderness. I can even spot a lone figure making their way across the terrain towards our camp, perhaps returning from an exploration or simply moving between sect

photos.caption: Morning frost — Looks like it was cold enough last night. It is so dry up there that you would not see any ice or frost to tell you it's very cold... you have to go to the water pipe!
photos.captionKii: Waking up or moving through the landscape, I came across this fascinating sight of water spraying from a source, creating these beautiful, intricate ice formations all around. The ground here was a mix of green grass and barren earth, and the icy patches really stood out against the rugged hillside. It's a vivid reminder of the cold temperatures we sometimes experienced even during the day in Patagonia, showcasing another unique aspect of this incredibly diverse environment we were exploring.

photos.caption: My Condo — Was pretty cold in my condo... but it was very causy with lots of room and lots of clothing layers.
photos.captionKii: I captured this quiet moment of a companion in their sleeping bag, nestled inside one of our tents during the Patagonian journey. They're wearing a bright green hooded jacket and have a headlamp on, its light reflecting softly in the darkness, hinting that it's late evening or early morning. There's a slight smile on their face, suggesting a comfortable rest after a day of exploring the rugged terrain. It was essential to capture these moments of downtime and recuperation, as they truly define the experience of expedition life.

photos.caption: Clouds from Confluencia camp
photos.captionKii: I was struck by this incredible view of the Patagonian mountains as the light faded, or perhaps began to rise. A massive, textured cloud, lit with stunning shades of pink and purple, seemed to drape itself over the snow-capped peaks, creating such a dramatic and ethereal scene. The rugged, dark mountains below, still holding their patches of snow, stood in stark contrast to the soft, colorful sky and the majestic cloud above. It was another one of those moments that truly captured the raw beauty and scale of this incredible region during our journey.

photos.caption: Clouds in fire
photos.captionKii: As another day drew to a close during my Patagonian journey, I couldn't help but capture this absolutely breathtaking sunset over the mountains. The sky was an incredible canvas of vivid orange, soft pinks, and deep purples, with the clouds illuminated by the setting sun. Below, the rugged peaks of the mountains stood in stark silhouette, their jagged edges and a few patches of snow still visible against the dramatic sky. It was a truly magnificent and serene moment, a perfect reminder of the raw, untouched beauty of this landscape.

photos.caption: Confluencia, an active village — At night the camp got quite full. If you expect some quiteness of a remote place... better go elsewhere. Here you got lots of people chatting, joking, shouting and even playing music (guitare, radio...). It'
photos.captionKii: This panoramic shot captures one of our main base camps in Patagonia, nestled against the impressive backdrop of the snow-capped mountains. You can see a wide array of tents, mostly yellow and orange, but also some blue and larger white dome tents, all spread out across the somewhat barren, rocky terrain. Above us, the sky was a dramatic canvas of clouds, making the rugged peaks look even more formidable. This was our home for a while, a place for rest and preparation amidst the wild beauty of the journey, and seeing all these tents together truly conveyed the scale of our expedition.

photos.caption: Confluencia -- visitors — More autonomous hikers got in later in the day. So I got lots of visit from people willing to chat. I even got quite a few birds coming along. In the "back-right" you can see Andre and Daniel, 2 guys from Quebec Ci
photos.captionKii: Back at one of our base camps in Patagonia, it was a busy scene with everyone getting things organized. I can see a couple of people near this grey dome tent, either setting it up or packing it away, while others are gathered further in the background, by what looks like another small structure with a 'Guardaparque' sign. What really caught my eye, besides the stunning rugged mountain backdrop, was this charming little bird with its yellow chest and grey-green feathers perched on a rock in the foreground, completely at home in this environment. It was another moment that truly encapsulated the

photos.caption:
Confluencia -- a lonely autonomous hiker condo — My tent stand alone in front of the huge amount of compagnies tent-mess...
It's amazing how few people go in this area in an autonomous way.
The camp managers even only save a quite small spa
photos.captionKii: This shot really brings me back to one of our base camps during the Patagonian journey. In the foreground, my orange MSR tent is open, inviting a peek inside where some of my gear is visible. Behind it, the camp sprawls out with rows of blue and yellow tents, all set against that incredibly dramatic backdrop of the snow-capped mountains. The ground was so rocky and rugged, but it was our home in the wilderness, providing essential shelter and a place to rest after those long days of exploring.

My friend on the left is making a funny, playful gesture with her fingers by her eyes, and I'm just beaming, happy to be sharing laughs and good company around the warm fire.

photos.caption: Rumney -  Scissor Dancing?
photos.captionKii: My friends and I captured this fun shot during our evening bonfire gathering. Here, two of us are making playful gestures with our fingers by our eyes, clearly enjoying the moment. One of my friends is wearing a headlamp, which was definitely useful as it was pitch black around us, highlighting the cozy, dark outdoor setting of our hangout. We were all smiles, sharing laughs and making memories that night.

photos.caption: Rumney - Party time — Little local early haloween fiesta at the Baker Campground
photos.captionKii: After a day out, I remember this cozy evening gathering around a crackling bonfire. It was pretty dark, but the flames cast a warm glow on everyone standing around, chatting and enjoying the night air. You can just make out a few chairs in the dim light, and it felt like such a relaxed way to unwind with friends, watching the embers glow and feeling the warmth against the crisp evening.

photos.caption: Rumney - Night friend — Climbing at night leads to some surprises
photos.captionKii: I got an even closer look at this tiny brown rodent, probably a vole, that we spotted during our climbing day. My finger is gently extended towards it, highlighting its small size and soft fur as it rests amongst the fallen leaves and dark ground. It was a really special moment to get so close to a wild creature during our adventure.

photos.caption: Rumney - Night friend — Little friend trying to hide at the crag's foot
photos.captionKii: While out in nature, I managed to capture this shot of a small, furry brown rodent, possibly a vole, nestled among the fallen leaves and twigs on the ground. It was quite dark, and the little creature blended in well with the mossy, rocky background, highlighting a quiet moment of wildlife observation during our outdoor adventure. It's always fascinating to see the smaller inhabitants of the woods.

photos.caption: Rumney — Parking log wall
photos.captionKii: I captured this shot of my friend in her bright orange jacket, looking up intently at the climbing route, perhaps spotting the next handhold or waiting for another climber higher up. Our loyal white and brown spotted dog is calmly sitting on the leafy ground right beside her, always a patient observer during our climbing adventures. In the background, another friend is also scanning the rock face. It's moments like these, surrounded by the beautiful autumn woods and good company, that make our climbing days at Rattlesnake Mountain so memorable.

photos.caption: Rumney — Parking lot wall
photos.captionKii: I captured this moment of a friend expertly belaying below a rocky cliff face while another climber makes their way up above. The belayer is wearing a bright orange jacket and harness, intently focused on the blue rope that secures the climber higher up, who is visible with a blue helmet. Our beautiful white and tan dog is patiently waiting near the belayer, adding to the adventurous yet peaceful atmosphere of our climbing day.

photos.caption: Rumney - Kiosk Map
photos.captionKii: I captured this detailed map of Rattlesnake Mountain, clearly showing various climbing areas like West Crags, Main Cliff, Orange Crush, and The Meadows. It's a fantastic guide, pointing out different climbing routes and features, and I can see specific elevations and names of crags. There's also some interesting text about the White Mountain National Forest and local flora and fauna. This map was essential for planning our adventure or recalling a great day spent exploring these climbing routes and understanding the lay of the land.

photos.caption: Rumney - Flying dog
photos.captionKii: I captured this lively moment of a beautiful, light-colored dog bounding through the dry grass and pine needles along the riverbank! The dog is in full stride, clearly enjoying the crisp autumn air. The river is calm, reflecting the clear sky, and the surrounding trees are starting to show those lovely fall colors, with some red foliage poking through. It was such a peaceful and picturesque day by the water, and seeing this energetic dog really added to the charm of the scene.

photos.caption: Rumney - View in Baker Campground
photos.captionKii: I captured this lovely scene of my friends enjoying a beautiful autumn day by a quiet river. One friend is standing, looking towards me with sunglasses on, holding her bright orange jacket, while another is casually sitting on the dry, pine-needle-strewn bank, bare-footed and relaxed. The water is calm and reflects the clear blue sky, and while many trees are still green, you can definitely see the rich fall colors starting to emerge on the distant hillsides, making for such a peaceful and picturesque outdoor moment.

photos.caption: Mt Pinacle - Mur de Zola's Slabs... rope is good!
photos.captionKii: This shot captures an amazing moment during an autumn rock climbing adventure! I can see someone, wearing an orange helmet and blue shirt, carefully rappelling down a massive, steep rock face. The view from up here is incredible, with the vibrant red and gold autumn leaves on the trees lining the cliff edge, and a wide, sparkling lake stretching out below. It really gives a sense of the scale of the climb and the beauty of the natural surroundings during fall.

photos.caption: Magali entering the Olympic stadium
photos.captionKii: I caught these runners in action, likely within an indoor section of the marathon or a covered pathway! The three participants are actively moving past a metal barrier, with a prominent red wall in the background. The woman in pink on the right is wearing a race bib with the number 5585 clearly visible, and the other two also have bibs, indicating they're part of the same event. It's cool to see the focus and determination on their faces as they make their way through this busy gathering.

photos.caption: Jerome going easy after 21km
photos.captionKii: I captured this bustling scene, which appears to be the finish area or an event expo, possibly indoors or under cover, for the 'Marathon international de Montréal' and 'Festival de la santé OASIS.' The prominent banners at the top, along with the digital clock displaying '1:36:23' and the 'OASIS' logo, confirm it's a major event. There are so many people around, some looking like participants in race bibs, others possibly spectators or staff. It feels like a vibrant hub of activity, marking the completion of a race or a central gathering point for the health festival.

photos.caption: Around the park

It's great to see so many people out participating in this active and positive gathering.

It's these everyday scenes of him feeling so at ease that I treasure.

photos.caption: Goin' for a run
photos.captionKii: This photo captures another sweet moment at home with our dog. My friend is bending down, giving our spotted dog some affectionate rubs, and he's got a big smile on his face looking at the camera. He's wearing a white and red athletic shirt, black shorts, and a grey 'HI-TEC' cap, looking very casual and happy. Our dog, with its distinctive white and brown spots, is eagerly nudging into his hands, clearly enjoying the attention. In the background, you can see our familiar tiled floor and a glimpse of a shoe rack and wooden cabinet, adding to the cozy, everyday atmosphere of our home.

photos.caption: Goin' for a run
photos.captionKii: This photo captures a really sweet moment with my friend on the tiled floor at home, giving a big hug to our spotted dog. My friend is squatting down, wearing a grey cap, a white and red athletic shirt with a black panel, and black shorts, looking at the camera with a slight smile. The dog, a lovely white and brown setter or spaniel, is being held close, looking a bit surprised, perhaps by the flash, but clearly enjoying the affection. In the background, you can see a wooden cabinet with some baskets and a pair of shoes neatly tucked away by the cabinet, adding to the cozy, indoor atmosphere.

photos.caption: Mauvaise herbe... 5.10d — Andrea nailed it!!!
photos.captionKii: This photo captures an amazing perspective from the base of a climb, looking way up at one of us (or a friend) high on the rock face against a brilliant blue sky. The sunlight highlights parts of the rugged rock, while shadows add depth and texture to the surface. You can see the vibrant green treetops framing the edges, emphasizing just how high up they are and the natural beauty of our surroundings. It really brings back the feeling of the immense scale and effort involved in these outdoor climbing adventures.

photos.caption: Mauvaise herbe... 5.10d — Andrea belaying Denis
photos.captionKii: This photo captures one of our climbing adventures, with a friend making their way up a steep rock face under the dappled sunlight. You can see the long shadows of the trees playing across the rugged rock, adding to the dramatic feel of the scene. Below, another friend is focused on belaying, ensuring everything is secure as the climber ascends. It was a beautiful day for some outdoor climbing, challenging ourselves against the natural terrain.

photos.caption: Escaping
photos.captionKii: I really enjoyed capturing this beautiful moment with a light pink and white morning glory flower. There's a small bee, with a striking greenish head, nestled deep inside, busy collecting pollen or nectar. The delicate veins of the flower are visible, and in the background, you can see some lush green leaves and another closed bud, waiting to unfurl. It's a peaceful scene, a reminder of the intricate beauty found in nature.

photos.caption: Happy one on Arabesque sport route's 2nd relay , ValDavid
